_readme: Real roster data decoded from paper rosters (Dec 2025 – June 2026). Use as unit-test fixtures for the generator and validators. June 2026 is complete for BOTH layers — the gold standard: a correct generator + the documented swaps should be able to produce rosters with these statistical properties, and the validators must report ZERO violations on these.

shoPool.note: returned from maternity leave June 2026; April/May pool was 3 people

shoRoster_2026_06.note: one person per day = on-call; flags on top. cash days == Pu days in consultantRoster_2026_06

nonCashWeekendRotation.carriedFromPreviousMonth: May last non-cash weekend = R, so June starts at M

shoRoster_2026_04_summary.note: April 2026 — pool of 3 (S, R, M; Udara on maternity). From the paper sheet's own tally box.
shoRoster_2026_04_summary.lesson: cash and post-cash inversely compensate (R: 3 cash → 1 post-cash; S: 2 → 3)

consultant6MonthSummary_Dec2025_May2026.lesson: cumulative fairness is tracked across months; imbalances (Pu 8 vs G 4 weekends) are what the app should surface and help correct

generatorAcceptanceTests: Validators report 0 violations when run on consultantRoster_2026_06 and shoRoster_2026_06
Consultant generator for a 4-weekend month assigns exactly 1 weekend block per consultant and 7-8 total days each
Consultant generator never produces back-to-back days (except within a weekend block), including against previousMonthTail
SHO generator's cash days exactly equal the consultant roster's Pu days
SHO generator assigns post-cash on the day after every cash day, to a different person, including across the month boundary
SHO generator: non-cash weekend = same person Sat+Sun (counts as 2 on-calls); cash weekend = 2 different people; following Monday = post-cash
SHO generator continues the non-cash weekend rotation from WeekendRotationState
5-weekend month: generator fills 4 weekend blocks and flags the 5th as needs-decision
No assignment lands on an UnavailabilityDate or outside activeFrom/activeUntil
